name: Senda del Oso (Desfiladero de Valdecerezales) — round trip from Entragu
name_original: Senda del Oso (Desfiladero de Valdecerezales) — ida y vuelta desde Entragu
region_intro: Jogging around Teverga offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by deep valleys, rugged gorges, and forested mountain slopes. The region features a mix of challenging ascents and scenic paths, often following ancient routes or riverbeds. Running trails in Teverga provide opportunities to explore both natural beauty and historical sites, with varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Teverga The most popular jogging route is Senda del Oso (Desfiladero de Valdecerezales) — round trip from Entragu, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 46 minutes to complete, winding through a scenic gorge. Another top favourite among local runners is Medieval Route of Alesga Castle Loop, a difficult 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This route explores historical landmarks and offers varied terrain around Alesga Castle. Local runners also love the Ricabo to Alto de Ventana Trail — circular via the GR 207, a 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with panoramic views, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Jogging around Teverga is defined by its deep valleys, challenging mountain trails, and historical routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to advanced running routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Teverga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy running routes in Teverga suitable for beginners?
A: While Teverga is known for its challenging mountain trails and varied terrain, offering 16 difficult routes, there are options for different fitness levels. The region does feature at least one easier path, allowing beginners to enjoy the scenic landscape. Most routes are designed for those seeking a more demanding running experience.

Q: What are some good options for long-distance running in Teverga?
A: For runners looking for longer distances, Teverga offers several challenging routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Valley – El Veneiru Teito Hut loop from Rio del Valle&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 40 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Somiedo – Braña de Mumián loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt;, covering approximately 30 km. These routes provide extensive mileage and significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along the running trails in Teverga?
A: Teverga's running routes often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You can explore deep valleys, rugged gorges, and forested mountain slopes. Notable natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2949141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Peñas Juntas on the Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, a dramatic gorge, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3592942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huerta Cave&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails follow ancient riverbeds, offering picturesque views.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, Teverga's trails blend natural beauty with historical significance. Runners can encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6332683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Braña de Mumián&lt;/a&gt;, an interesting historical site, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5520289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Tower of Proaza&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240525709/medieval-route-of-alesga-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Route of Alesga Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores historical landmarks around Alesga Castle.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Teverga region?
A: The Teverga region offers a diverse network of nearly 20 running routes. These trails vary in length and difficulty, providing options for different preferences and fitness levels, from shorter scenic paths to challenging long-distance mountain runs.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Teverga?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Teverga, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the region's diverse landscape, which includes deep valleys, rugged gorges, and forested mountain slopes. The mix of challenging ascents and scenic paths, often following ancient routes or riverbeds, is particularly appreciated.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Teverga?
A: Yes, Teverga features several circular running routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240516498/ricabo-to-alto-de-ventana-trail-circular-via-the-gr-207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricabo to Alto de Ventana Trail — circular via the GR 207&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 16.7 km trail offering panoramic views through mountainous terrain. Circular routes are ideal for those who prefer to start and end their run at the same point.

Q: Can I find running trails that are suitable for families in Teverga?
A: While many routes in Teverga are rated as difficult, the region's diverse network includes paths that can be enjoyed by families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6736914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a well-known trail that offers a generally accessible experience, though specific sections may vary in difficulty. It's advisable to check individual route details for suitability with children.

Q: Are there any running routes in Teverga that feature gorges or canyons?
A: Absolutely. Teverga is characterized by its rugged gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240578876/senda-del-oso-desfiladero-de-valdecerezales-round-trip-from-entragu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso (Desfiladero de Valdecerezales) — round trip from Entragu&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, winding through a scenic gorge. You can also experience the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2949141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Peñas Juntas on the Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5971245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso Tunnel (Valdecerezales Gorge)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on running trails in Teverga?
A: The terrain in Teverga is highly varied, ranging from ancient routes and riverbeds to challenging mountain paths. You'll encounter deep valleys, rugged gorges, and forested slopes. Expect a mix of ascents and descents, often on unpaved trails, making for a dynamic and engaging running experience.

Q: Are there any advanced running routes for experienced runners in Teverga?
A: Yes, Teverga is particularly well-suited for advanced runners, with 16 out of its nearly 20 routes rated as difficult. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Valley – El Veneiru Teito Hut loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240516498/ricabo-to-alto-de-ventana-trail-circular-via-the-gr-207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricabo to Alto de Ventana Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and long distances, providing a substantial challenge for experienced athletes.
tips: The Teverga Gorges are an impressive part of the Senda del Oso, a very popular hiking and cycling route in Asturias. This trail follows the route of an old mining railway and crosses the Valles del Oso, which include the municipalities of Quirós, Santo Adriano, Proaza and Teverga.

The Bear Trail Tunnel, also known as the Valdecerezales Gorge, is one of the highlights of the Bear Trail in Asturias. This tunnel runs through an impressive gorge, offering a unique experience for hikers and cyclists who follow the route.
